pattern: Tracking the smallest and second-smallest values seen so far detects a third larger value in one pass with constant space.
hints: You only need to find three increasing values by index, not necessarily adjacent.
Keep the best candidate for the first of the triple and the best for the second so a third can complete it.
Maintain two running minima: update first when a value is no larger than it, else update second; if a value exceeds both, a triple exists.

edge_inputs.description: strictly increasing has an obvious triple
triple exists despite a dip in the middle
too short to hold a triple
duplicates do not count as increasing
two increasing then a stall, no third
negative to positive crossing forms a triple

stress.description: random arrays over a small range, often with and without triples
stress.note: narrow range produces a balanced mix of true and false cases
